annotation_basis: Blind annotation from source O&M text only.

concept_ground_truth.label: T1
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: For Helion PackCore-544 AWD, capture whether the issue at the front manifold face is coolant odor, slow level loss, or wetness after recent manifold removal, and note the service history that may have altered bolt clamp load or seal compression. Record the manifold-side residue pattern before the first bolt is touched.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T1 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T2
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Safe the pack and remove the front manifold cover so the composite manifold body, M6 retaining bolts, steel inserts, upper and lower hose necks, perimeter O-ring channel, and nearby NTC loom can be seen together. The manifold and its adjacent hose loads need to be read in one view because a distorted hose path can change flange compression.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T2 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T3
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Inspect bolt witness paint, washer seating, flange gap, and dried coolant crystals around the full perimeter, then compare the upper-left and lower-right corners where clamp load often diverges first. A local flange opening at one boss points to a different repair boundary than even residue around the entire channel.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T3 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T4
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Lift the manifold only enough to inspect the EPDM O-ring cross-section and the groove corners at the inlet crossover, checking for twist, flattening, nicked lips, or dry installation marks. Keep dirt out of the aluminum seal land so the later compression evidence remains reliable.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T4 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T5
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Lay the manifold back against the face and measure flange stand-off at several bolts while watching whether one hose neck or bracket pushes the body off the header plate. If the top hose must be forced downward to start the bolt, the boundary is likely hose-induced preload or manifold warp rather than the seal alone.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T5 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T6
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Inspect the plastic body for bow, the steel inserts for pull-up, and the bolt stack for extra washer thickness or uneven bolt stretch so clamp loss is assigned to a real mechanical cause. This prevents the same leak from being mislabeled as a generic O-ring failure.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T6 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T7
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Replace or reseat only the failed manifold, O-ring, bolt stack, or hose support item, then torque the manifold in sequence from the center outward and confirm that the flange gap closes evenly at all corners. The hose necks should sit naturally with no side pull on the composite body.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T7 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T8
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Pressure-test and circulate the restored manifold branch while monitoring the manifold face, hose spigots, and the nearby NTC temperature trend because a trapped air pocket or warped flange can reopen only after the plastic body warms. Record both the dry condition and the thermal behavior during this proof cycle.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T8 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T9
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Mark the manifold repair complete only when the record identifies the exact failure mode, such as twisted perimeter O-ring, warped composite flange, insert pull-up, overlength bolt stack, or hose-side preload, and ties it to the repeated pressure and circulation result. The release boundary is a manifold face that stays dry at all bosses with even clamp witness and stable temperature response across the repaired front branch.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T9 retained verbatim from the source document.
